A special form of climate change is abrupt climate change.
A negatively recoupled system will thus compensate for disturbances in its energy balance and return to the original climate state.

Although the importance of the other greenhouse gases is quite relevant in view of the concise climate change events of the past, in addition to water vapour, almost exclusively carbon dioxide and methane played a primary role, since they currently have almost the same global warming potential as carbon dioxide in their overall effect.

In contrast to previous assumptions, the additional anthropogenic CO2 emissions will only gradually decrease, even with a large-scale emission stoppage, and can still be detected significantly in several thousand years, since carbon dioxide in the Earth's atmosphere is degraded very slowly by natural physical and biogeochemical processes in the Earth's system.
